text: The average VO2max increase after 20 weeks of standardized exercise training in sedentary adults is approximately 400 ml/min.
Individual VO2max training responses are highly heterogeneous, ranging from little or no gain to increases exceeding 1.0 l/min on the same standardized program.
Between-family variance in VO2max training response is 2.5 times greater than within-family variance, indicating strong familial aggregation of trainability.
The heritability of VO2max trainability is estimated at 47%, meaning roughly half of individual differences in training response are attributable to genetic factors.
Maternal transmission accounts for an estimated 28% of the familial component of VO2max trainability, suggesting mitochondrial DNA or maternal environmental effects.

A non-responder to one training program may not be a true non-responder — genetics constrain the magnitude of VO2max adaptation, so coaching should individualize volume and intensity rather than assume uniform responses.
